Wait to call TaskOutput until either: - If you want to check on the agent's progress - call TaskOutput with block=false to get an immediate update on the agent's status - If you run out of things to do and the agent is still running - call TaskOutput with block=true to idle and wait for the agent's result (do not use block=true unless you completely run out of things to do as it will waste time).

1→"""Prepare impl-context for the current worktree's PR. 2→ 3→Sets up .erk/impl-context/ folder from the PR associated with the current 4→branch, making the worktree ready for plan implementation. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk pr prepare # Auto-detect plan number from current branch's PR 8→ erk pr prepare 1234 # Explicit plan number 9→""" 10→ 11→import click 12→ 13→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.setup_impl_from_pr import create_impl_context_from_pr 14→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 15→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 16→from erk.core.repo_discovery import NoRepoSentinel, RepoContext 17→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRNotFound 18→from erk_shared.impl_folder import read_plan_ref, resolve_impl_dir 19→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 20→ 21→ 22→@click.command("prepare") 23→@click.argument("plan_number", type=int, required=False, default=None) 24→@click.pass_context 25→def pr_prepare(ctx: click.Context, plan_number: int | None) -> None: 26→ """Set up impl-context for the current worktree's PR. 27→ 28→ Prepares the current worktree for plan implementation by creating 29→ the .erk/impl-context/ folder with plan content from the PR. 30→
... [60 lines omitted] ...

Add a PAT with 'repo' scope at: Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions" 230→ if git ls-files --error-unmatch .erk/impl-context/ >/dev/null 2>&1; then 300→ continue-on-error: true 372→ continue-on-error: true 392→ - name: Handle merge conflicts if push failed 394→ if: steps.submit.outcome == 'failure' 424→ if git ls-files --error-unmatch .erk/impl-context/ >/dev/null 2>&1; then 434→ continue-on-error: true 470→ - name: Fail workflow if implementation failed 473→ echo "Implementation failed with exit code: ${{ steps.implement.outputs.exit_code }}"

1→name: one-shot 2→run-name: "one-shot${{ inputs.plan_only && '(plan)' || '' }}:#${{ inputs.pr_number }}:${{ inputs.distinct_id }}" 3→ 4→on: 5→ workflow_dispatch: 6→ inputs: 7→ prompt: 8→ description: "Task description for autonomous execution" 9→ required: true 10→ type: string 11→ branch_name: 12→ description: "Branch to work on (created by CLI command)" 13→ required: true 14→ type: string 15→ pr_number: 16→ description: "Draft PR number (created by CLI command)" 17→ required: true 18→ type: string 19→ submitted_by: 20→ description: "GitHub username of the person who submitted" 21→ required: true 22→ type: string 23→ distinct_id: 24→ description: "Unique identifier for run discovery (base36)" 25→ required: true 26→ type: string 27→ model_name: 28→ description: "Claude model to use for execution" 29→ required: false 30→ type: string

82→ echo "::error title=Missing Secret::ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT secret is not configured or has expired. Add a PAT with 'repo' scope at: Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions" 168→ echo "::warning::Plan rejected: $REASON" 174→ echo "::error::Planning succeeded but .erk/impl-context/plan.md not found" 178→ echo "::error::Planning succeeded but .erk/impl-context/plan-result.json not found" 218→ continue-on-error: true 269→ if git ls-files --error-unmatch .erk/impl-context/prompt.md >/dev/null 2>&1; then 272→ if git ls-files --error-unmatch .worker-impl/prompt.md >/dev/null 2>&1; then 286→ - name: Fail workflow if planning failed 289→ echo "One-shot planning failed with exit code: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.exit_code }}"

1→--- 2→description: Create a plan from a one-shot prompt, save it as a draft PR, and write results to .erk/impl-context/ (used by CI workflow) 3→--- 4→ 5→# One-Shot Plan 6→ 7→You are running autonomously in a CI workflow. Your job is to read a prompt, explore the codebase, create a detailed implementation plan, and save it as a draft PR. 8→ 9→**Important:** You are ONLY planning, not implementing. The plan must be self-contained — a separate Claude session will implement it with no access to your exploration context. 10→ 11→## Step 1: Read the Prompt 12→ 13→Read `.erk/impl-context/prompt.md` to understand what you need to do. 14→ 15→## Step 2: Read Objective Context (if present) 16→ 17→If the `$OBJECTIVE_ISSUE` environment variable is set (non-empty), this plan is for a specific node of an objective. Use `erk exec check-objective $OBJECTIVE_ISSUE` to fetch the full objective context (title, roadmap, progress). The `$NODE_ID` environment variable contains the specific roadmap node ID being planned. Incorporate the objective context into your planning — understand the broader goal and how this node fits into it. 18→ 19→## Step 3: Load Context 20→ 21→Read `AGENTS.md` to understand the project conventions. Follow its documentation-first discovery process: scan `docs/learned/index.md`, grep `docs/learned/` for relevant docs, and load skills as directed by AGENTS.md routing rules. 22→ 23→## Step 4: Explore the Codebase 24→ 25→Use Explore agents and Grep/Glob to understand the relevant areas of the codebase: 26→ 27→- Search for files, patterns, and existing implementations related to the prompt 28→- Identify which files need to be created or modified 29→- Understand existing architecture and patterns 30→- Find relevant tests and test patterns
... [73 lines omitted] ...
60→Parse the JSON output. If `success` is not `true`, stop and report the error. Otherwise, use `$PLAN_ISSUE_NUMBER` as the `plan_number`. To get the `title`, extract the first `# ` heading from `.erk/impl-context/plan.md`. 78→Parse the JSON output. If `success` is not `true`, stop and report the error. Otherwise, extract `plan_number` and `title` from the output.

1→--- 2→description: CI-only skill for autonomous learn plan consolidation (used by consolidate-learn-plans workflow) 3→--- 4→ 5→# Consolidate Learn Plans (CI) 6→ 7→You are running autonomously in a CI workflow. Your job is to query all open erk-learn plans, consolidate them into a single implementation-ready documentation plan, and output the results. 8→ 9→**Important:** This is the autonomous CI version. No user interaction (no AskUserQuestion). If there are no plans, output a sentinel and exit cleanly. 10→ 11→## Step 1: Load Context 12→ 13→Read `AGENTS.md` to understand the project conventions. Follow its documentation-first discovery process: scan `docs/learned/index.md`, grep `docs/learned/` for relevant docs. 14→ 15→## Step 2: Query Open erk-learn Plans 16→ 17→Fetch all open plans with the `erk-learn` label: 18→ 19→```bash 20→gh api repos/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY:-dagster-io/erk}/issues \ 21→ -X GET \ 22→ --paginate \ 23→ -f labels=erk-learn \ 24→ -f state=open \ 25→ -f per_page=100 \ 26→ --jq '.[] | {number, title, created_at, labels: [.labels[].name]}' 27→``` 28→ 29→## Step 3: Filter Out Already-Consolidated Plans 30→
... [110 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→description: Update an existing plan with the current session's plan 3→argument-hint: <plan-number> 4→--- 5→ 6→# /local:plan-update 7→ 8→Update an existing plan with the current session's plan content. 9→ 10→## Usage 11→ 12→```bash 13→/local:plan-update 42 14→/local:plan-update https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/42 15→``` 16→ 17→--- 18→ 19→## Agent Instructions 20→ 21→### Step 1: Parse Plan Number 22→ 23→Extract the plan number from the argument: 24→ 25→- If numeric (e.g., `42`), use directly 26→- If URL (e.g., `https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/42`), extract the number from the path 27→ 28→If no argument provided, check `ref.json` in `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` for a linked plan number. 29→ 30→If still no plan number, ask the user for the plan number.
... [38 lines omitted] ...
48→- If `branch_name` is set but `branch_updated` is false: `"Plan updated on PR #N (warning: branch push failed)"` 50→On failure, display the error and suggest:

1→--- 2→description: Extract insights from plan-associated sessions 3→argument-hint: "[plan-number]" 4→--- 5→ 6→# /erk:learn 7→ 8→Create a documentation plan from Claude Code sessions associated with a plan implementation. The verb "learn" means: analyze what happened, extract insights, and create an actionable plan to document those learnings. 9→ 10→## Usage 11→ 12→``` 13→/erk:learn # Infers plan from current branch 14→/erk:learn 4655 # Explicit plan number 15→``` 16→ 17→## Purpose 18→ 19→All documentation produced by this command follows the content quality standards in the `learned-docs` skill. Load that skill for the full rules on audience (AI agents), the cornerstone principle (cross-cutting insight), and what belongs in learned docs vs code comments. 20→ 21→## Agent Instructions 22→ 23→**Prerequisite:** Load the `learned-docs` skill for content quality standards before proceeding. 24→ 25→Tell the user (choose the appropriate pipeline display based on whether `.erk/impl-context/` exists): 26→ 27→**When `.erk/impl-context/` exists (CI/async mode):** 28→ 29→``` 30→Learn pipeline for plan #<plan-number> (using preprocessed materials):
... [802 lines omitted] ...
55→Parse the JSON output and check the `labels` array. If `erk-learn` is present, stop with this error: 58→Error: Plan #<plan-number> is a learn plan (has erk-learn label). 143→This returns JSON with PR details (`number`, `title`, `state`, `url`, `head_ref_name`, `base_ref_name`) or an error if no PR exists. Save the PR number for the parallel agents below. 414→Confirm you see the expected files (session-\*.md, diff-analysis.md, existing-docs-check.md, pr-comments-analysis.md) before proceeding. If any files are missing, the agent failed to write its output and must be re-launched. 545→If agents were not launched or failed, fall back to manual analysis. 574→- Error messages and how they were resolved 743→Parse the JSON output. If `count` is 0, no comment was added (no candidates found by the extractor). This is normal and not an error. 759→**If plan validation failed (no actionable documentation):** 825→- `<tool_result>` elements with errors often reveal the most useful insights

Always valid — numeric guaranteed .claude/skills/dignified-python/dignified-python-core.md:457: raw_id = data.get("plan_id") .claude/skills/dignified-python/dignified-python-core.md:460: return PlanRef(plan_id=raw_id, url=data["url"], provider=data["provider"]) .claude/skills/dignified-python/dignified-python-core.md:468: plan_id: str # session.assistant: Now I can see the full scope. Let me re-read the plan more carefully to understand what's in scope for this PR:
session.tool_result:
1→ 1→# Plan: Rename Click Arguments/Options (Objective #9109, Node 2.3) 2→ 2→ 3→ 3→Part of Objective #9109, Node 2.3: Rename Click arguments/options: plan_number -> pr_number, plan_id -> pr_number across CLI commands. 4→ 4→ 5→ 5→## Context 6→ 6→ 7→ 7→Objective #9109 is renaming "plan" terminology to "pr" across all APIs. Phases 1 and 2.1-2.2 are done (core types, gateway ABCs, JSON output fields, TypedDict fields). Node 2.3 renames the Click argument/option declarations and their corresponding function parameter names. This must also update all callers (workflows, skills, commands) that pass these CLI flags, to prevent breakage. 8→ 8→ 9→ 9→## Scope 10→ 10→ 11→ 11→**In scope:** Click decorators, function parameter names, internal variable usage, and all callers of the renamed flags. 12→ 12→ 13→ 13→**Out of scope:** Help strings and error messages (node 2.4), skill/command documentation prose (node 4.2). 14→ 14→ 15→ 15→## Rename Mapping 16→ 16→ 17→ 17→| Current CLI Flag | Current Param | New CLI Flag | New Param | 18→ 18→|-----------------|---------------|-------------|-----------| 19→ 19→| `--plan-number` | `plan_number` | `--pr-number` | `pr_number` | 20→ 20→| `--plan-id` (int) | `plan_id` | `--pr-number` | `pr_number` | 21→ 21→| `--plan-id` (str) | `plan_id` | `--pr-id` | `pr_id` | 22→ 22→| positional `plan_number` | `plan_number` | positional `pr_number` | `pr_number` | 23→ 23→| positional `plan_id` (int) | `plan_id` | positional `pr_number` | `pr_number` | 24→ 24→| `--plan` / `-p` | `plan` | `--plan` / `-p` | `plan` (keep - this is admin test helper) | 25→ 25→ 26→ 26→**Special cases:** 27→ 27→- `update_plan_header.py`: `plan_id` is `type=str` -> rename to `pr_id` (str) 28→ 28→- `dispatch_cmd.py`: `plan_numbers` (tuple) -> `pr_numbers` (tuple), CLI name `plan_numbers` positional -> `pr_numbers` 29→ 29→- `admin.py`: `--plan` / `-p` option is for an admin test command, rename to `--pr` / `-p` 30→ 30→
... [80 lines omitted] ...
90→ 90→**Note:** Do NOT rename error message text or help strings - those are node 2.4. 94→ 94→1. Run `ruff check` to verify no syntax errors
session.assistant:
Good. Now I have a clear picture of the full plan. The plan says `track_learn_result.py` maps `--plan-id` (int) -> `--pr-number`, but looking at the actual code it's `type=str`. Let me re-read the plan mapping more carefully:
- Line 44: `track_learn_result.py` - `plan_id` param -> `pr_number`
But in the actual file, `--plan-id` is `type=str`. The plan says rename to `pr_number` though. Let me check the plan mapping table again: - `--plan-id` (int) → `--pr-number` / `pr_number` - `--plan-id` (str) → `--pr-id` / `pr_id`
So `track_learn_result.py` (which is `type=str`) should actually become `--pr-id` / `pr_id`. But the plan line 44 says `pr_number`. This is a plan inconsistency. Since `track_learn_result.py` uses `plan_id: str` (passed as `"--plan-id"` with `type=str`), the correct rename per the mapping table is `--pr-id` / `pr_id`.
Let me check on the background agents now:

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/store_tripwire_candidates.py","old_string":"@click.command(name="store-tripwire-candidates")
@click.option("--plan-number", required=True, type=int, help="Plan number")
@click.option("--candidates-file", required=True, help="Path to tripwire-candidates.json")
@click.pass_context
def store_tripwire_candidates(
    ctx: click.Context,
    *,
    plan_number: int,
    candidates_file: str,
) -> None:","new_string":"@click.command(name="store-tripwire-candidates")

@click.option("--candidates-file", required=True, help="Path to tripwire-candidates.json")
@click.pass_context
def store_tripwire_candidates(
    ctx: click.Context,
    *,
    pr_number: int,
    candidates_file: str,
) -> None:"}

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py","old_string":"@click.command(name="register-one-shot-plan")
@click.option("--plan-number", type=int, required=True)
@click.option("--run-id", type=str, required=True)
@click.option("--pr-number", type=int, required=True)
@click.option("--submitted-by", type=str, required=True)
@click.option("--run-url", type=str, required=True)
@click.pass_context
def register_one_shot_plan(
    ctx: click.Context,
    *,
    plan_number: int,
    run_id: str,
    pr_number: int,
    submitted_by: str,
    run_url: str,
) -> None:","new_string":"@click.command(name="register-one-shot-plan")
@click.option("--pr-number", type=int, required=True)
@click.option("--run-id", type=str, required=True)
@click.option("--impl-pr-number", type=int, required=True)
@click.option("--submitted-by", type=str, required=True)
@click.option("--run-url", type=str, required=True)
@click.pass_context
def register_one_shot_plan(
    ctx: click.Context,
    *,
    pr_number: int,
    run_id: str,
    impl_pr_number: int,
    submitted_by: str,
    run_url: str,
) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py","old_string":"    # Op 1: dispatch metadata
    try:
        write_dispatch_metadata(
            plan_backend=plan_backend,
            github=github,
            repo_root=repo_root,
            plan_number=plan_number,
            run_id=run_id,
            dispatched_at=datetime.now(UTC).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
        )
        results["dispatch_metadata"] = {"success": True}
    except Exception as exc:
        results["dispatch_metadata"] = {"success": False, "error": str(exc)}","new_string":"    # Op 1: dispatch metadata
    try:
        write_dispatch_metadata(
            plan_backend=plan_backend,
            github=github,
            repo_root=repo_root,
            plan_number=pr_number,
            run_id=run_id,
            dispatched_at=datetime.now(UTC).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
        )
        results["dispatch_metadata"] = {"success": True}
    except Exception as exc:
        results["dispatch_metadata"] = {"success": False, "error": str(exc)}"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py","old_string":"    # Op 2: queued comment
    try:
        issue = issues.get_issue(repo_root, plan_number)
        if isinstance(issue, IssueNotFound):
            raise RuntimeError(f"Plan #{plan_number} not found")
        # Extract owner/repo from run_url for the PR link
        url_parts = run_url.split("/")
        repo_slug = next(
            (
                f"{url_parts[i + 1]}/{url_parts[i + 2]}"
                for i, p in enumerate(url_parts)
                if p == "github.com" and i + 2 < len(url_parts)
            ),
            "",
        )
        issues.add_comment(
            repo_root,
            plan_number,
            f"\U0001f504 **Queued for Implementation**\n\n"
            f"**Submitted by:** @{submitted_by}\n"
            f"**PR:** [#{pr_number}](https://github.com/{repo_slug}/pull/{pr_number})\n"
            f"**Workflow run:** [View run]({run_url})\n",
        )
        results["queued_comment"] = {"success": True}
    except Exception as exc:
        results["queued_comment"] = {"success": False, "error": str(exc)}","new_string":"    # Op 2: queued comment
    try:
        issue = issues.get_issue(repo_root, pr_number)
        if isinstance(issue, IssueNotFound):
            raise RuntimeError(f"Plan #{pr_number} not found")
        # Extract owner/repo from run_url for the PR link
        url_parts = run_url.split("/")
        repo_slug = next(
            (
                f"{url_parts[i + 1]}/{url_parts[i + 2]}"
                for i, p in enumerate(url_parts)
                if p == "github.com" and i + 2 < len(url_parts)
            ),
            "",
        )
        issues.add_comment(
            repo_root,
            pr_number,
            f"\U0001f504 **Queued for Implementation**\n\n"
            f"**Submitted by:** @{submitted_by}\n"
            f"**PR:** [#{impl_pr_number}](https://github.com/{repo_slug}/pull/{impl_pr_number})\n"
            f"**Workflow run:** [View run]({run_url})\n",
        )
        results["queued_comment"] = {"success": True}
    except Exception as exc:
        results["queued_comment"] = {"success": False, "error": str(exc)}"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py","old_string":"    # Op 3: update lifecycle stage to "planned"
    try:
        plan_backend.update_metadata(
            repo_root,
            str(plan_number),
            metadata={"lifecycle_stage": "planned"},
        )
        results["lifecycle_stage"] = {"success": True}
    except Exception as exc:
        results["lifecycle_stage"] = {"success": False, "error": str(exc)}","new_string":"    # Op 3: update lifecycle stage to "planned"
    try:
        plan_backend.update_metadata(
            repo_root,
            str(pr_number),
            metadata={"lifecycle_stage": "planned"},
        )
        results["lifecycle_stage"] = {"success": True}
    except Exception as exc:
        results["lifecycle_stage"] = {"success": False, "error": str(exc)}"}

        plan_backend.update_metadata(

            str(pr_number),

        )

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/post_workflow_started_comment.py","old_string":"    block = create_workflow_started_block(
        started_at=started_at,
        workflow_run_id=run_id,
        workflow_run_url=run_url,
        plan_number=plan_number,
        branch_name=branch_name,
    )

    description = (
        f"Setup completed successfully.\n"
        f"\n"
        f"**Branch:** `{branch_name}`\n"
        f"**PR:** [#{pr_number}](https://github.com/{repository}/pull/{pr_number})\n"
        f"**Status:** Ready for implementation\n"
        f"\n"
        f"[View workflow run]({run_url})"
    )","new_string":"    block = create_workflow_started_block(
        started_at=started_at,
        workflow_run_id=run_id,
        workflow_run_url=run_url,
        plan_number=pr_number,
        branch_name=branch_name,
    )

    description = (
        f"Setup completed successfully.\n"
        f"\n"
        f"**Branch:** `{branch_name}`\n"
        f"**PR:** [#{impl_pr_number}](https://github.com/{repository}/pull/{impl_pr_number})\n"
        f"**Status:** Ready for implementation\n"
        f"\n"
        f"[View workflow run]({run_url})"
    )"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/post_workflow_started_comment.py","old_string":"@click.command(name="post-workflow-started-comment")
@click.option("--plan-number", type=int, required=True, help="Plan identifier")
@click.option("--branch-name", type=str, required=True, help="Git branch name")
@click.option("--pr-number", type=int, required=True, help="Pull request number")
@click.option("--run-id", type=str, required=True, help="GitHub Actions workflow run ID")
@click.option("--run-url", type=str, required=True, help="Full URL to workflow run")
@click.option("--repository", type=str, required=True, help="Repository in owner/repo format")
@click.pass_context
def post_workflow_started_comment(
    ctx: click.Context,
    *,
    plan_number: int,
    branch_name: str,
    pr_number: int,
    run_id: str,
    run_url: str,
    repository: str,
) -> None:","new_string":"@click.command(name="post-workflow-started-comment")
@click.option("--pr-number", type=int, required=True, help="Plan identifier")
@click.option("--branch-name", type=str, required=True, help="Git branch name")
@click.option("--impl-pr-number", type=int, required=True, help="Pull request number")
@click.option("--run-id", type=str, required=True, help="GitHub Actions workflow run ID")
@click.option("--run-url", type=str, required=True, help="Full URL to workflow run")
@click.option("--repository", type=str, required=True, help="Repository in owner/repo format")
@click.pass_context
def post_workflow_started_comment(
    ctx: click.Context,
    *,
    pr_number: int,
    branch_name: str,
    impl_pr_number: int,
    run_id: str,
    run_url: str,
    repository: str,
) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/post_workflow_started_comment.py","old_string":"    # Build comment body
    now_iso = time.now().replace(tzinfo=UTC).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    comment_body = _build_workflow_started_comment(
        plan_number=plan_number,
        branch_name=branch_name,
        pr_number=pr_number,
        run_id=run_id,
        run_url=run_url,
        repository=repository,
        now_iso=now_iso,
    )

    # Post comment via PlanBackend (handles both issue and planned-PR plans)
    try:
        backend.add_comment(repo_root, str(plan_number), comment_body)
        result = PostSuccess(success=True, pr_number=plan_number)
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))
    except RuntimeError as e:
        result = PostError(
            success=False,
            error="github-api-failed",
            message=str(e),
        )
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))
        raise SystemExit(1) from e","new_string":"    # Build comment body
    now_iso = time.now().replace(tzinfo=UTC).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    comment_body = _build_workflow_started_comment(
        pr_number=pr_number,
        branch_name=branch_name,
        impl_pr_number=impl_pr_number,
        run_id=run_id,
        run_url=run_url,
        repository=repository,
        now_iso=now_iso,
    )

    # Post comment via PlanBackend (handles both issue and planned-PR plans)
    try:
        backend.add_comment(repo_root, str(pr_number), comment_body)
        result = PostSuccess(success=True, pr_number=pr_number)
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))
    except RuntimeError as e:
        result = PostError(
            success=False,
            error="github-api-failed",
            message=str(e),
        )
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))
        raise SystemExit(1) from e"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/post_workflow_started_comment.py","old_string":"    # Build comment body
    now_iso = time.now().replace(tzinfo=UTC).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    comment_body = _build_workflow_started_comment(
        plan_number=plan_number,
        branch_name=branch_name,
        pr_number=pr_number,
        run_id=run_id,
        run_url=run_url,
        repository=repository,
        now_iso=now_iso,
    )

    # Post comment via PlanBackend (handles both issue and planned-PR plans)
    try:
        backend.add_comment(repo_root, str(plan_number), comment_body)
        result = PostSuccess(success=True, pr_number=plan_number)
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))
    except RuntimeError as e:
        result = PostError(
            success=False,
            error="github-api-failed",
            message=str(e),
        )
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))
        raise SystemExit(1) from e","new_string":"    # Build comment body
    now_iso = time.now().replace(tzinfo=UTC).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    comment_body = _build_workflow_started_comment(
        pr_number=pr_number,
        branch_name=branch_name,
        impl_pr_number=impl_pr_number,
        run_id=run_id,
        run_url=run_url,
        repository=repository,
        now_iso=now_iso,
    )

    # Post comment via PlanBackend (handles both issue and planned-PR plans)
    try:
        backend.add_comment(repo_root, str(pr_number), comment_body)
        result = PostSuccess(success=True, pr_number=pr_number)
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))
    except RuntimeError as e:
        result = PostError(
            success=False,
            error="github-api-failed",
            message=str(e),
        )
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))
        raise SystemExit(1) from e"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_update.py","old_string":"@click.command(name="plan-update")
@click.option(
    "--plan-number",
    type=int,
    required=True,
    help="Plan number to update",
)
@click.option(
    "--format",
    "output_format",
    type=click.Choice(["json", "display"]),
    default="json",
    help="Output format: json (default) or display (formatted text)",
)
@click.option(
    "--plan-path",
    type=click.Path(exists=True, path_type=Path),
    help="Direct path to plan file (overrides session lookup)",
)
@click.option(
    "--session-id",
    help="Session ID to find plan file in scratch storage",
)
@click.option(
    "--summary",
    help="AI-generated summary to display above the collapsed plan in the PR body",
)
@click.pass_context
def plan_update(
    ctx: click.Context,
    *,
    plan_number: int,
    output_format: str,
    plan_path: Path | None,
    session_id: str | None,
    summary: str | None,
) -> None:","new_string":"@click.command(name="plan-update")
@click.option(
    "--pr-number",
    type=int,
    required=True,
    help="Plan number to update",
)
@click.option(
    "--format",
    "output_format",
    type=click.Choice(["json", "display"]),
    default="json",
    help="Output format: json (default) or display (formatted text)",
)
@click.option(
    "--plan-path",
    type=click.Path(exists=True, path_type=Path),
    help="Direct path to plan file (overrides session lookup)",
)
@click.option(
    "--session-id",
    help="Session ID to find plan file in scratch storage",
)
@click.option(
    "--summary",
    help="AI-generated summary to display above the collapsed plan in the PR body",
)
@click.pass_context
def plan_update(
    ctx: click.Context,
    *,
    pr_number: int,
    output_format: str,
    plan_path: Path | None,
    session_id: str | None,
    summary: str | None,
) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_update.py","old_string":"    # Step 2: Check plan exists via PlanBackend
    plan_id = str(plan_number)
    plan_result = backend.get_plan(repo_root, plan_id)
    if isinstance(plan_result, PlanNotFound):
        _handle_update_error(f"Plan #{plan_number} not found")","new_string":"    # Step 2: Check plan exists via PlanBackend
    plan_id = str(pr_number)
    plan_result = backend.get_plan(repo_root, plan_id)
    if isinstance(plan_result, PlanNotFound):
        _handle_update_error(f"Plan #{pr_number} not found")"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_update.py","old_string":"    # Step 6: Output success
    if output_format == "display":
        click.echo(f"Plan #{plan_number} updated")
        click.echo(f"Title: {full_title}")
        click.echo(f"URL: {plan_result.url}")
        if branch_updated:
            click.echo(f"Branch {branch_name} synced")
        elif branch_name is not None:
            click.echo(f"Warning: branch push to {branch_name} failed")
    else:
        click.echo(
            json.dumps(
                {
                    "success": True,
                    "pr_number": plan_number,
                    "pr_url": plan_result.url,
                    "title": full_title,
                    "branch_name": branch_name,
                    "branch_updated": branch_updated,
                }
            )
        )","new_string":"    # Step 6: Output success
    if output_format == "display":
        click.echo(f"Plan #{pr_number} updated")
        click.echo(f"Title: {full_title}")
        click.echo(f"URL: {plan_result.url}")
        if branch_updated:
            click.echo(f"Branch {branch_name} synced")
        elif branch_name is not None:
            click.echo(f"Warning: branch push to {branch_name} failed")
    else:
        click.echo(
            json.dumps(
                {
                    "success": True,
                    "pr_number": pr_number,
                    "pr_url": plan_result.url,
                    "title": full_title,
                    "branch_name": branch_name,
                    "branch_updated": branch_updated,
                }
            )
        )"}

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_update.py","old_string":"    # Step 6: Output success
    if output_format == "display":
        click.echo(f"Plan #{plan_number} updated")
        click.echo(f"Title: {full_title}")
        click.echo(f"URL: {plan_result.url}")
        if branch_updated:
            click.echo(f"Branch {branch_name} synced")
        elif branch_name is not None:
            click.echo(f"Warning: branch push to {branch_name} failed")
    else:
        click.echo(
            json.dumps(
                {
                    "success": True,
                    "pr_number": plan_number,
                    "pr_url": plan_result.url,
                    "title": full_title,
                    "branch_name": branch_name,
                    "branch_updated": branch_updated,

        )","new_string":"    # Step 6: Output success
    if output_format == "display":
        click.echo(f"Plan #{pr_number} updated")
        click.echo(f"Title: {full_title}")
        click.echo(f"URL: {plan_result.url}")
        if branch_updated:
            click.echo(f"Branch {branch_name} synced")
        elif branch_name is not None:
            click.echo(f"Warning: branch push to {branch_name} failed")
    else:
        click.echo(
            json.dumps(
                {
                    "success": True,
                    "pr_number": pr_number,
                    "pr_url": plan_result.url,
                    "title": full_title,
                    "branch_name": branch_name,
                    "branch_updated": branch_updated,
                }
            )
        )"}
- All error message prose text was preserved (only variable references within f-strings were updated)
session.assistant: Agent 1 completed but made an error - it renamed `--pr-number` to `--impl-pr-number` in `register_one_shot_plan.py` and `post_workflow_started_comment.py`. Those files had BOTH `--plan-number` and `--pr-number` already, and the plan says only `--plan-number` → `--pr-number`. The existing `--pr-number` should NOT be renamed. I need to fix this. Let me check on the other agents and then fix:
